rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF WAUKEEN MCCOY's Motion For Leave To Amend Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint DENIED. This action is stayed. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1324(b). Moving party failed to request a leave to file a supplemental complaint. Moving party fails to explain the effect of the amendments on the remaining defendants and causes of action alleged against them. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: WAUKEEN Q. MCCOY VS. KEN PAGE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Corrected Notice Of Pending Action
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF WAUKEEN MCCOY's Motion For Leave To File Corrected Notice Of Pending Action DENIED. This action is stayed. Remaining (not-settled) causes of action do not assert a real property claim as to the remaining defendants. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SERGEI KORJAVIN-URYSON VS. AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the causes of action for constructive eviction and breach of contract for Plaintiff to allege that he vacated the premises and specific express terms of the contract that have been breached. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Complaint sufficiently alleges landlord-tenant relationship (paragraphs 7, 2); notice re: repairs; failure to repair. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SERGEI KORJAVIN-URYSON VS. AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 9, 2014, line 5. DEFENDANT MATTHEW MUATH ZGHOUL's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts re: conscious disregard for Plaintiff health and safety. = (501/REQ)
